SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog

Record Class ScanArgs

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.ScanArgs
Record Components:
filters - filter expressions for potential pushdown, or null if no filter information is available. An empty list means filters were evaluated but none apply.
projection - column indices to include in the scan results, or null for all columns. An empty list means zero columns are explicitly requested (e.g., for count(*)).
limit - maximum number of rows to return, or null for no limit
public record ScanArgs(List<Expr> filters, List<Integer> projection, Long limit) extends Record
Arguments for a table scan operation.

Contains the filter expressions, column projection, and row limit that DataFusion passes to a TableProvider when creating an execution plan.

Example: 

@Override
public ExecutionPlan scanWithArgs(Session session, ScanArgs args) {
    List<Integer> proj = args.projection(); // columns to read, or null for all
    Long limit = args.limit(); // max rows, or null for no limit
    List<Expr> filters = args.filters(); // pushdown filters, or null
    return new MyExecutionPlan(schema(), proj, limit);
}
See Also:

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
